steps.title: Characterize the approach of each text
steps.content:
Text 1 reports Nicholson and colleagues’ findings in a neutral, factual register — listing Nadir’s observable features (elevated rim, circular shape, terraced floor, central uplift) and stating their impact-crater interpretation without commentary. Text 2 takes a more engaged approach: it considers alternative explanations and dismisses them before arriving at the impact interpretation, showing the reasoning process rather than just the conclusion.

The question asks which choice best describes how these two approaches differ.

steps.title: Evaluate each choice
steps.content:
• Choice A — ‘Text 1 dispassionately describes Nicholson and colleagues' findings and conclusions, whereas Text 2 attempts to convey the researchers' excitement on discovering Nadir.’: The first clause is accurate; Text 1 is purely descriptive. The second clause uses “excitement” loosely to capture Text 2’s more engaged, investigative tone. Of all four options, this one alone correctly identifies that Text 1 is neutral description while Text 2 involves more active engagement. ✓
  • Choice B — ‘Text 1 focuses on features Nadir lacks, whereas Text 2 indicates features it shares with other geological depressions.’: Text 1 lists features Nadir has, not features it lacks. The premise is wrong. ✗
  • Choice C — ‘Text 1 discusses a single plausible cause of Nadir, whereas Text 2 evaluates two possible causes.’: Text 1 does not frame itself as evaluating causes at all — it simply reports findings. The contrast described here does not match either text accurately. ✗
  • Choice D — ‘Text 1 emphasizes the evidence supporting an asteroid impact as the cause of Nadir, whereas Text 2 argues against that explanation.’: Text 2 ultimately supports the impact explanation; it does not argue against it. ✗
That confirms A is correct.
